title: Complex Facial Laceration
chief_complaint: Car accident. Deep cut on my face from broken glass. I'm worried about scarring.

physical_exam.Face: 7cm laceration through left cheek extending from nasal ala to angle of mandible. Deep: Through skin, subcutaneous tissue, and into muscle (zygomaticus major). Jagged edges. Glass fragments visible.
physical_exam.Neurologic: Facial nerve: Can raise eyebrow, close eyes tightly, smile symmetrically (CN VII intact). Infraorbital nerve: Sensation decreased in left cheek and upper lip (neuropraxia from contusion - likely temporary).
physical_exam.Salivary: Parotid duct: Clear fluid from wound? No. Parotid gland not involved.
physical_exam.Eyes: No globe injury. Normal vision. No orbital fracture on CT.

hidden_data.ct_face: No facial fractures. No orbital injury. No intracranial injury. Glass fragments in soft tissue of left cheek.
hidden_data.tetanus: Tetanus status: Last booster 8 years ago (needs updated).

correct_diagnosis: Complex Full-Thickness Facial Laceration (Left Cheek) with Muscle Involvement and Retained Foreign Bodies
key_learning_points: Facial laceration principles: 1) Tetanus prophylaxis (if >5 years since last booster for contaminated wounds). 2) Antibiotics: Indicated for contaminated wounds, through-and-through oral lacerations, animal/human bites (Amoxicillin-Clavulanate). 3) Timing: Primary closure within 6-8 hours (face has rich blood supply, can close up to 24h if clean). 4) Debridement: Remove devitalized tissue, foreign bodies. Conservative debridement on face (preserve tissue). 5) Irrigation: Copious NS irrigation (200-500mL) under pressure (19G angiocath + 35mL syringe)

Layered closure: Deep layers: Absorbable suture (Vicryl, Monocryl) for muscle (3-0 or 4-0) and dermis (4-0 or 5-0). Skin: Non-absorbable monofilament (Nylon, Prolene 5-0 or 6-0) in interrupted sutures. Remove skin sutures in 5-7 days (face heals faster). Evert skin edges to prevent depressed scar

Special considerations: Vermilion border (lip): First suture to align border exactly (1mm mismatch visible). Eyebrow: Do NOT shave (hair may not regrow). Align hair follicles. Eyelid: 6-0 or 7-0 suture. Cartilage (ear, nose): Suture perichondrium, not cartilage alone. Parotid duct: If injured, repair over stent (22G angiocath) - consult plastics/ENT. Facial nerve: If transected, primary neurorrhaphy under microscope - consult plastics/ENT emergently

Post-care: Keep wound clean and dry. Apply antibiotic ointment (Bacitracin). Avoid sun exposure (hyperpigmentation of scar). Silicone gel/sheets after suture removal (reduces scar hypertrophy). Scar revision if needed after 6-12 months (once mature)
